**Current Price:** $415 (as of March 30, 2026 close)

**Bottom Line:** The 30-day distribution is **Markov-model driven** (no trusted GLD-specific Polymarket anchors available). Median forecast is flat at $414.33 (−0.2%), with asymmetric tails favoring downside.

---

### Key Probability Levels

| GLD Price | P(> Price) | Interpretation |
|-----------|------------|----------------|
| $380      | 69.1%      | Strong support floor |
| $397      | 61.5%      | ~4% downside protection |
| $410      | 53.4%      | Near current price |
| $422      | 45.0%      | modest upside |
| $434      | 36.9%      | +4.5% gain |
| $447      | 29.5%      | +7.6% target |
| $461      | 23.1%      | +11% bull case |
| $475      | 17.8%      | +14.5% stretch |
| $500+     | 10.2%      | Low-probability moonshot |

---

### Scenario Summary

| Scenario | Probability | Price Level | Return |
|----------|-------------|-------------|--------|
| **Bear** (80% prob) | 20% | $366 | −11.8% |
| **Median** | 50% | $414 | −0.2% |
| **Target** (30% prob) | 70% | $447 | +7.6% |
| **Bull** (20% prob) | 80% | $469 | +13.0% |

**Model Signal:** HOLD/SELL bias — 41.9% chance of >3% decline vs. 36.1% chance of >5% gain

---

### ⚠️ Important Limitations

1. **No Polymarket anchors:** The distribution is 100% Markov-model driven (historical regime transitions). Gold-specific prediction markets exist but map imperfectly to GLD ETF shares.
2. **Medium confidence:** Sparse sideways regime observations make transitions prior-dominated.
3. **Geopolitical premium:** Current gold price embeds Middle East conflict risk ($102+ oil). De-escalation could trigger mean reversion to $375-385 GLD.

GLD-specific Polymarket markets (e.g., "Will GLD exceed $450 by April 30?") would anchor the distribution with real-money crowd probabilities. Current gold futures markets ($/oz) don't map cleanly to the ETF.

**Trade implication:** The distribution suggests **range-bound with downside skew**. If holding, consider protective puts below $380. If entering, wait for pullback to $390-400 for better risk/reward.

**Signal:** 📈 **BUY** | Expected return: +2.7% | Risk/reward: 2.31×

---

### Key Probability Levels

| Price Level | P(> Price) | Interpretation |
|-------------|------------|----------------|
| $60,000 | 94.4% | Strong floor — only 5.6% chance of breach |
| $64,000 | 92.7% | High confidence support |
| $68,000 | 67.0% | Current price sits near 33rd percentile |
| $71,000 | 45.3% | Near-median upside target |
| $74,000 | 12.4% | Bull case threshold (1 in 8 chance) |
| $82,000 | 2.8% | Low probability breakout |

---

### Scenario Breakdown

| Scenario | Probability | Price Target | Return |
|----------|-------------|--------------|--------|
| Bear case (80% confidence) | — | $66,319 | −3.2% |
| Median forecast | — | $70,365 | +2.7% |
| Target (30% prob) | 30% | $72,423 | +5.7% |
| Bull case (20% prob) | 20% | $73,319 | +7.0% |
| Stop-loss (90% confidence) | — | $65,119 | −5.0% |

**Actionable view:** 35.2% chance of >5% gain | 43.5% chance of range-bound (±3-5%) | 21.3% chance of >3% loss

---

### Model Diagnostics

- **Regime:** Bull
- **Anchor quality:** SPARSE (5 trusted Polymarket markets)
- **Mixing:** 100% Polymarket anchors / 0% Markov (anchors dominate due to limited 30-day markets)
- **⚠️ Caveats:**
  - Sparse anchors — most 30-day interpolation is model-driven
  - Structural break detected (divergence=0.426) — confidence intervals widened 50%
  - Most Polymarket markets expire April 1-6, not true 30-day horizon

---

### Bottom Line

The crowd-implied distribution is **tightly range-bound** with a modest bullish tilt. There's a 90% probability BTC trades between **$65K–$74K** over the next 30 days. The asymmetry favors upside (2.31× risk/reward), but conviction is limited by sparse long-dated prediction markets.

**Trade implication:** Small long position near $68.5K makes sense with stop at $65.1K and target $72.4K. Avoid leverage — range-bound regimes punish forced positioning.

**Current Price:** $415 (as of March 30, 2026 close)

**Bottom Line:** The 30-day distribution is **Markov-model driven** (no trusted GLD-specific Polymarket anchors available). Median forecast is flat at $414.33 (−0.2%), with asymmetric tails favoring downside.

| Scenario | Probability | Price Level | Return |
|----------|-------------|-------------|--------|
| **Bear** (80% prob) | 20% | $366 | −11.8% |
| **Median** | 50% | $414 | −0.2% |
| **Target** (30% prob) | 70% | $447 | +7.6% |
| **Bull** (20% prob) | 80% | $469 | +13.0% |

**Model Signal:** HOLD/SELL bias — 41.9% chance of >3% decline vs. 36.1% chance of >5% gain

---

### ⚠️ Important Limitations

1. **No Polymarket anchors:** The distribution is 100% Markov-model driven (historical regime transitions). Gold-specific prediction markets exist but map imperfectly to GLD ETF shares.
2. **Medium confidence:** Sparse sideways regime observations make transitions prior-dominated.
3. **Geopolitical premium:** Current gold price embeds Middle East conflict risk ($102+ oil). De-escalation could trigger mean reversion to $375-385 GLD.

---

### What Would Improve This Forecast

GLD-specific Polymarket markets (e.g., "Will GLD exceed $450 by April 30?") would anchor the distribution with real-money crowd probabilities. Current gold futures markets ($/oz) don't map cleanly to the ETF.

**Trade implication:** The distribution suggests **range-bound with downside skew**. If holding, consider protective puts below $380. If entering, wait for pullback to $390-400 for better risk/reward.
